threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a good idea to bring a suitcase to Dalston Junction instead of a backpack. That hotel vibe won't fly here.
Last night on the N37, a guy pulled out a saxophone and played ‘Careless Whisper’ while we all sat in silence. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Listening to the morning buskers on Karl-Marx-Strasse, one strumming a ukulele while a toddler twirled, made me realize this is home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a flea market where a DJ spins vinyl while locals haggle over retro furniture. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they casually mention the BART delays during Friday dinner plans like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling on the 19 Polk this morning. He had a sign saying 'life’s a circus'—and honestly, it felt like it for everyone onboard.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'tapas' means sharing plates. Locals actually prefer 'platillos'—smaller, solo servings, so you can savor each one.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, while the locals glide right through.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot newcomers by how they still think the bus is the fastest way to get anywhere; seasoned locals know it's all about timing the Overground.
Caught a guy on the 333 singing R&B to an empty seat like it was his personal stage. Only in Brixton, I swear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw the neighbor’s cat perched on the bus stop sign, oblivious to the chaos around it. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a kebab stand open at 3 AM selling vegan döner that actually tastes like the real deal. It's a late-night miracle!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they can spot a tourist by their baffled expression at BART delays—it's like recognizing an old friend after a long absence.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a rider serenading the Muni with a saxophone while stuck in traffic. Only in the TL would you find music amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all love the loud music at the Festa Major, but locals often just want a quiet evening on the balcony.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still look amazed at the Sagrada Família's façade, while locals just rush past, phone glued to their ear.
